Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・修験堂内
English: February 9 Inner Temple Training Hall

Japanese: 成歩堂。これが、 ゴドー検事の言っていた‥‥？
English: Wright, is this what Godot was talking about?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。《からくり錠》だよ。
English: ...Yeah. The trick locks.

Japanese: それでは、被告人！ さっそく、解除をおねがいしたい。
English: Now then, Iris. Please remove these at once.

Japanese: あ‥‥あの‥‥検事さま。 ‥‥申しわけありません。
English: Umm... Mr. Edgeworth... I'm afraid I can't...

Japanese: 私には‥‥不可能、です。
English: It-It's not possible for me...

Japanese: ‥‥なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: （さっきの地震‥‥修験洞が崩れる のは、まぬがれたが‥‥）
English: (During the earthquake, when the cavern was in danger of caving in, Iris escaped.)

Japanese: （ぼくが見たとき、 《錠》は‥‥１個だけだった）
English: (And I know that there was only one lock when I last came here.)

Japanese: 増えた錠の解除は、できない‥‥ そういうこと、かしら。
English: So you're saying that you can't undo the new locks?
...Yes.

Japanese: わ、私としたことが‥‥ こんな不手際を‥‥ッ！
English: If only I was stronger...!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣、大丈夫か？ カオがまっ青だぞ。
English: Edgeworth, how are you feeling? You look a little pale in the face.

Japanese: ミドリ色のキミに 言われたくないな。
English: Like you're one to talk with your face all green.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣　怜侍。 少し、風に当たってきなさい。
English: ...Miles Edgeworth. Go and get some air.

Japanese: 被告人は、私が見ておくわ。 アタマを冷やすの。
English: I'll watch over the suspect. You go and get a grip on yourself.

Japanese: バカな！　私は‥‥‥ うむッ！
English: Don't be ridiculous! I'm perfectly finngh!

Japanese: ‥‥今のあなたは、どんな ミスを重ねるかわからない。
English: There's no telling what sort of mistakes you could make in your current state.

Japanese: 早く回復するの！　‥‥それが、 あなたのシゴトよ。御剣　怜侍！
English: Go and get some rest! That's your only concern now, Miles Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥‥わかった。
English: Understood.

Japanese: 私は、中庭で捜査の指揮をとる。 ‥‥ここは、キミにまかせよう。
English: I'll handle the investigation in the garden... You take care of things here.
(Edgeworth...)

Japanese: アイツ、プライドが高いからな。 中庭のスミで泣いてるぞ、きっと。
English: He's got so much pride that he's probably off crying in a corner of the garden...

Japanese: プライドなんて、生きてゆくには ジャマなだけのシロモノよ。
English: Pride is simply another trap that hinders us in our lives.

Japanese: でも‥‥いいシゴトをするため には、なくてはならない。
English: That said... One must have pride to be effective on the job.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、あなたと行動を 共にするのも、ここまでね。
English: At any rate, it seems that this is where we part ways, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 私は、やっかいなパズル遊びに つきあうから。
English: I'm going to stay here and see if I can't help solve these bothersome puzzles.

Japanese: ‥‥そう、か。 ありがとう。
English: ...I see. Well, thanks for your help.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥お話を聞かせて もらえますか？　あやめさん。
English: Now then... Do you mind if I ask you a few things, Iris?

Japanese: はい‥‥。
English: No, not at all...

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら‥‥ やっと、見えてきたな‥‥）
English: (I think I'm finally beginning to see how the pieces fit together...)

Japanese: ‥‥検事という立場上‥‥ すべて、聞かせてもらったわ。
English: ...I have asked her everything I can in my capacity as a prosecutor.

Japanese: この事件‥‥
English: This incident...

Japanese: すべては、綾里家の物語を 舞台にした事件だったのね。
English: Everything related to it goes back to the history of the Fey clan.

Japanese: ‥‥そうみたい、だね。
English: ...That's what it looks like.

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。もう１つだけ‥‥ 教えてほしいことがあります。
English: Iris... There's just one more thing I want you to tell me.

Japanese: なんでしょう‥‥？
English: What is it...?

Japanese: お姉さん‥‥美柳　ちなみが 弁護士に毒を盛った事件で‥‥
English: During the incident in which your sister, Dahlia Hawthorne, poisoned a lawyer...

Japanese: 彼女は、証拠品をかくすために、 ある男子学生と交際を始めました。
English: ...she began relations with a certain college student in order to hide the evidence.

Japanese: その学生について‥‥ 何か、聞いていませんか？
English: That college student... Have you heard anything about him...?

Japanese: おっしゃってましたわ。 ‥‥ヒトコトだけ。
English: Well... I did hear one thing...

Japanese: “うっとうしいヤツ”‥‥と。
English: She said she hated his guts.

Japanese: そうですか。 ‥‥ありがとうございます。
English: ...I see. Thank you for your help.

Japanese: どういたしまして‥‥。
English: You're welcome...

Japanese: さあ、急ぐの、成歩堂　龍一！ まだ、調べることがあるはずよ。
English: Hurry up, Phoenix Wright! There is still much to investigate!

Japanese: この錠は、おまかせください。 かならず、解除いたします。
English: Leave these locks to me. I'll open them for you, I promise.

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいします。 （とにかく‥‥今は前へ進もう！）
English: Thank you. (I suppose I'd better continue my investigation.)

Japanese: （まだ、１つ‥‥ 《秘密》が残されている）
English: (There's still one giant secret left to unlock...)

Japanese: （‥‥そう。春美ちゃんの 《サイコ・ロック》が‥‥）
English: (Pearls's Psyche-Lock...)

Japanese: （倉院流霊媒道の家元、 綾里　舞子が描かれた、掛け軸）
English: (This scroll shows a picture of Misty Fey, Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique.)

Japanese: （そこに、なぜか‥‥ カレーがぶちまけられている）
English: (But why would anyone cover it in gravy?)

Japanese: ‥‥これじゃあ、何が 描いてあるのかわからないわ。
English: I can't make out what's drawn on this scroll at all.

Japanese: この国の芸術家のやるコトは、 リカイできないわね。
English: I'll never understand you Americans and your so-called artists...

Japanese: （ゲージュツ、ねえ‥‥）
English: (Artists? Um, yeah...)

Japanese: 古ぼけたタンスだ。
English: It's an antique dresser.

Japanese: 勝手に開けちゃダメよ、 成歩堂　龍一！
English: Don't you dare open it, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （なんか、真宵ちゃんに なった気分だな‥‥）
English: (Now I know how Maya feels when I tell her not to touch things...)

Japanese: なんだ。修験者の装束しか 入っていないわ。
English: What a pity. It's just full of old clothes for the acolytes.

Japanese: ‥‥勝手に開けちゃ ダメなんじゃなかったのかよ。
English: I thought we weren't opening it!

Japanese: 私は検事局の人間なのよ。 ナニをやってもユルされるの。
English: I'm from the Prosecutor's Office. I can do anything.

Japanese: （‥‥ショーゲキ的な発言だな）
English: (Yeah, you can do anything... Except stand up to a 9 year old girl.)

Japanese: ‥‥これは何かしら？ 葉桜院にもあったけど。
English: What is this? There was one in the Main Hall as well.

Japanese: “火ばち”っていうんだ。 ストーブみたいなもの、かな。
English: It's a kind of hearth. It's called a "hibachi".

Japanese: へえ、おもしろいわ。
English: Really? How interesting.

Japanese: ちょっと、火をつけてみて。 成歩堂　龍一。
English: Light it for me, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ぼく、使い方は知らないんだ。
English: I-I don't know how...

Japanese: いてててッ！
English: Ouch!

Japanese: キサマのようなクサったワカゾウが 文化の火に水をかけるのだッ！
English: Dim-witted morons like you are to blame for the cultural decay that blots our future!

Japanese: ‥‥今度、ベンキョウしておくよ。 （“文化”と言われると弱い）
English: I-I'll look into it and learn! (*sniffle* Now even Maya is more cultured than me...)

Japanese: このトビラ‥‥
English: Hmm... This door...

Japanese: ２日前に来たときは、こんな 妙な《錠》は、かかってなかった。
English: When I was here earlier, there was only one lock, but now there's five of them.

Japanese: まるで、ほら穴を守っているかの ようね。‥‥この、クサリ。
English: Those chains... It's almost as if they're guarding something inside that cavern.

Japanese: こんなフシギな《錠》‥‥ 見たことがないわ。
English: I've never seen locks quite like these before.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくには、見おぼえがある。 この《クサリ》と《錠》‥‥）
English: (I have. I've seen locks and chains just like these before.)

Japanese: （ココロの暗闇を守る 《サイコ・ロック》‥‥で）
English: (They look just like the ones that guard a person's secrets during a Psyche-Lock.)

Japanese: （この《錠》も、何かを かくしているのだろうか？）
English: (I wonder if these locks are guarding something, too.)

Japanese: （《ほら穴》という 暗闇の向こうに‥‥）
English: (Dark secrets in a dark cavern...)

Japanese: せんべいブトンが たたまれている。
English: Look at those wafer-thin mattresses!

Japanese: ちょっと待ちなさい、 成歩堂　龍一。
English: I'm sorry, Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: 何かしら？ 《せんべいブトン》って。
English: "Wafer-thin"?

Japanese: ウスくてペッタリした、 おせんべいみたいなフトンだよ。
English: Yeah, you know. Thin like a wafer. In other words, uncomfortable.

Japanese: この国じゃ、そう言うの。
English: That's how we describe things like these mattresses.

Japanese: アメリカで言えば‥‥ 《ビスケットタオルケット》かな。
English: I guess you must say, "wafer- thick" or something back in Germany?

Japanese: そ、そうなの。 ‥‥勉強になったわ。
English: Not about these mattresses. Certainly not!

Japanese: それにしても‥‥ どうして脱走したんですか？
English: Why did you make a run for it, Iris?

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。本当に、 申しわけありませんでした。
English: I... I'm sorry.

Japanese: 奥の院は、かなり地盤が揺らいで いると聞いていたモノですから‥‥
English: I heard the Inner Temple had been severely shaken by the strong earthquake we had.

Japanese: 私‥‥シンパイになってしまって。 ムチュウで、ここへ‥‥
English: I... I was so worried. I just had to come and see.

Japanese: 自由のために逃げたのではない‥‥ それは、よくわかったわ。
English: In other words, you didn't run away to escape the law. At least we're clear on that.

Japanese: 修験洞がブジで、私‥‥ ホッとしました。‥‥でも。
English: I can't tell you how relieved I was when I saw the Sacred Cavern was alright, but...
But what?

Japanese: ‥‥ごらんのとおりです。
English: Then I saw these chains here.

Japanese: 何者かが、こんなに厳重な 《錠》で、修験洞を‥‥
English: I saw all these extra locks that someone had put on the Sacred Cavern's door, and...

Japanese: ぬうううう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: いったい、どなたが こんなコトを‥‥
English: Who in the world would've done something like this?

Japanese: この《からくり錠》は、倉院に 伝わる秘宝の１つで‥‥
English: These trick locks are a sacred treasure of the Kurain Tradition.

Japanese: 錠を閉ざす方法が、 数百通り存在します。
English: There are hundreds of ways to set them.

Japanese: だから‥‥解除できるのは、 その錠をかけた人間だけなのです。
English: That's why only the person who set the lock can open it.

Japanese: でも、これをセットしたのは あなたではないのかしら？
English: And you aren't the one who set these locks?

Japanese: 話は、そんなに カンタンじゃないよ、狩魔　冥。
English: I don't think it's that simple, Franziska von Karma.

Japanese: ‥‥最初、ぼくたちが見たとき カギは、１つだけだった。
English: When we were here the first time, there was only one lock.

Japanese: それが、いつの間にか、 ５つに増えた‥‥
English: But now, somehow, there's five of them...

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ どういうことなのですか？
English: What does that mean?

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさんが ここへ到着する前に‥‥
English: It means that someone wanted to secure the place even more...

Japanese: ‥‥だれかが、 錠を増やしたんです。
English: And they wanted to secure it before you got here, Iris.

Japanese: たぶん‥‥真宵ちゃんを 閉じこめておくために！
English: Presumably because they wanted to make sure Maya couldn't get out.

Japanese: あやめさんが解除できる《錠》は ‥‥はじめの１個だけです。
English: This means that Iris can only open one of these locks. The first one.

Japanese: たしかに、そう‥‥ですね。
English: Yes... That's correct.

Japanese: なんてこと‥‥
English: What?

Japanese: あやめさん！　なんとか ならないんですか！
English: Iris! Try to think, please! Isn't there any way around this?

Japanese: カギをかける方法は、 数百通りですから‥‥
English: Well, like I said, there are hundreds of different ways to set these locks.

Japanese: ひとつずつ試せば、解除することは 可能‥‥です。
English: I suppose if I went through every combination with each one, I could remove them.

Japanese: ただ‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: 時間がかかるわね。
English: It will take time, won't it?

Japanese: ええ‥‥。まる１日ぐらい かかると思います。
English: Yes. About a day, if I had to guess.

Japanese: （ま‥‥まる１日！）
English: (A whole day!?)

Japanese: ‥‥開かないよりはマシよ。 おねがいできるかしら？
English: Well, that's better than leaving the locks in place. Will you do this for us?

Japanese: はい。できるかぎりのことは やらせていただきます。
English: Sure. I'll do whatever I can.

Japanese: （あと１日、か‥‥）
English: (We've got to wait another day?)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥ なんとか、がんばってくれ！）
English: (Hang in there, Maya. You're going to have to call on your inner strength now.)

Japanese: そういえば‥‥あやめさん。
English: You know what, Iris...

Japanese: まだ、ハッキリしていない ことが１つ、ありましたね。
English: There's still one thing I don't quite get.

Japanese: なんのことかしら？ 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: And what might that be, Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥
English: I think it's obvious.

Japanese: 事件当夜、あやめさんが どこにいたか‥‥ですよ。
English: Iris, on the night of the murder, where were you?

Japanese: いやいや！ “？”じゃないでしょう。
English: Please, Iris! Don't give me that look!

Japanese: あなた自身は、事件が起こるまで、 葉桜院の自室にいたと言ってます。
English: You told us that you were in your room at Hazakura Temple at the time of the incident.

Japanese: でも‥‥あの晩、奥の院で あなたを目撃した人物がいる。
English: But you were seen that same evening at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: さらにあなたは、葉桜院で、 《犯行現場》を目撃されている‥‥
English: And then, you were spotted at the scene of the crime in Hazakura Temple, too.

Japanese: 《葉桜院》と《奥の院》‥‥ まるで、被告人が２人いるみたい。
English: Being spotted at both Hazakura Temple and the Inner Temple; it's as if you were...

Japanese: いかがですか？　あやめさん。 そろそろ、本当のことを‥‥
English: Well, Iris? I think it's about time you told us the truth.

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり‥‥何か、 事情があるらしいな‥‥）
English: (I knew it. There's something going on here that we don't know about.)

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: 事件当夜、あなたは ２つの場所で目撃されています。
English: On the night of the murder, you were spotted in two very different locations:

Japanese: 《葉桜院の境内》と、 《奥の院の修験堂》‥‥
English: In Hazakura Temple's courtyard, and in the Training Hall at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: あなた自身は、葉桜院から 出なかったと言っています。
English: And yet you claim that you were in your room the entire time.

Japanese: でも‥‥言いにくいけど、 それでは、ナットクできない。
English: I'm sorry, Iris, but I don't buy your story.

Japanese: あやめさん。‥‥そろそろ、 本当のことを聞かせてください！
English: It's time you told me the truth!

Japanese: 申しわけありません、 成歩堂さま‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （‥‥前から、気になっていた ことが、もう１つある‥‥）
English: (...Truthfully, there's one other thing that's been bothering me.)

Japanese: （このサイコ・ロックが 解除できれば‥‥）
English: (If I can just break this Psyche-Lock...)

Japanese: （すべてがハッキリするの かもしれない！）
English: (...I might be able to answer that question as well.)

Japanese: 私は‥‥あの晩、 葉桜院の部屋におりました。
English: I... I was in my room in the Main Hall that night.

Japanese: 奥の院で、もう１人の私が 目撃されたと聞いて‥‥
English: As soon as I heard that I'd been spotted at the Inner Temple, I knew.

Japanese: すぐに、お姉さまだと わかりましたの。
English: I knew it was my sister.

Japanese: 先月‥‥お亡くなりになった ちなみさま、だと‥‥。
English: Dahlia, the other half of me who... passed away last month.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (Just as I suspected.)

Japanese: （事件当夜、奥の院でビキニさんが 見た《あやめさん》は‥‥）
English: (The "Iris" that Sister Bikini saw at the Inner Temple on the night of the murder...)

Japanese: （美柳　ちなみだったんだ！）
English: (It was Dahlia Hawthorne!)

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ！　すぐに 教えてくれなかったんですか！
English: Why didn't you tell me this before!?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥‥‥
English: B-Because...

Japanese: お姉さまのなさることは、 いつでも、正しいからですわ。
English: Because my sister always does the right thing.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥？
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: それに‥‥私に、お姉さまを ジャマすることはできませんの。
English: And because I mustn't get in the way of what she's trying to do.

Japanese: だって、私は‥‥ うらぎりもの、なのですから。
English: I already betrayed her once before! I can't do it again!

Japanese: う‥‥“うらぎりもの”‥‥ あなたが、ですか？
English: You betrayed her? What do you mean?

Japanese: だから‥‥私。有罪になっても しかたがないんです。
English: That's why I... I have to accept I may be found guilty. It's the only way.

Japanese: （ど‥‥‥ どういうことだ、いったい‥‥）
English: (What is she talking about!?)

Japanese: 成歩堂さま‥‥ご存じですね。
English: You know about it, don't you, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 今から１０年ほど前。この吾童山で 起こった、狂言誘拐事件を‥‥
English: About the fake kidnapping that took place here on Eagle Mountain 11 years ago?

Japanese: ‥‥あの事件がキッカケで、 運命は、大きく狂ってしまった‥‥
English: That was the start of it all. It twisted her entire destiny.

Japanese: お姉さまは、次々に罪を重ね‥‥ ついには、命を落とすことに‥‥
English: She started to commit crime after crime, and in the end, she... she lost her life.

Japanese: すべては‥‥この私が ウラ切ったせい、なのです。
English: I-It's all because I betrayed her!

Japanese: あ、あやめさんが‥‥？
English: How did you betray her, Iris?

Japanese: 身代金の受け渡し場所に、吾童山が 選ばれたのは、偶然ではなかった。
English: It was no coincidence that Eagle Mountain is where the exchange was to take place.

Japanese: 最初の計画では、私が‥‥ お手伝いするはずでしたの。
English: After all, I... I helped plan the whole thing.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: でも‥‥私、コワかった。 だから、逃げてしまったのです。
English: But I got scared, so I ran away.

Japanese: な、何を言ってるんですか！ 協力しないのは、当然ですよ！
English: What are you talking about!? Why would you help her!?

Japanese: ２億円を奪うなんて‥‥それも、 自分の父親から！　最低です！
English: Stealing 2 million dollars... from your own father! That's awful!

Japanese: でも‥‥私、一度は 協力するとヤクソクしたのです。
English: But I promised. I promised that I'd help.

Japanese: それに‥‥
English: And...

Japanese: お姉さまは、２億円が ほしかったわけではありませんの。
English: She didn't do it for the money.
Huh?

Japanese: あの、誘拐は‥‥ 私たちの父への復讐だったのです。
English: It was revenge. On our father.

Japanese: ふ‥‥《復讐》ですって‥‥？
English: R-Revenge?

Japanese: お父さんへの‥‥復讐？
English: What do you mean by revenge...?

Japanese: そうです。おかあさまを捨てて 地獄へ追いやった、あの男への‥‥
English: He was a hideous man. He threw our mother away, and then sent her to Hell...

Japanese: （“おかあさま”‥‥ 綾里　キミ子のこと、か‥‥）
English: (Her mother? She must be talking about Morgan Fey...)

Japanese: 私たち双子は、倉院流本家・ 長女の娘として生まれました。
English: Our mother was the eldest daughter of the main branch of the Fey family.

Japanese: 当時の倉院流は、政財界とつながり が強く、家元の権力は絶大でした。
English: The main family had a lot of influence in many business and political circles at the time.

Japanese: 長女であるおかあさまは、 家元の座が約束されていたのです。
English: As the eldest daughter, our mother was set to inherit all of that as the next Master.

Japanese: 私たちの父は、そのチカラを 手にするため、母に近づいた。
English: That's the reason our father married our mother in the first place. For power.

Japanese: でも‥‥その思惑は、 大きくハズれることになります。
English: But his plan backfired.

Japanese: ‥‥おかあさまは‥‥妹に、 家元の座を奪われてしまった。
English: Because our mother's sister took it all from her. She took over as the Master of Kurain.

Japanese: （それが、綾里　舞子さん‥‥ 真宵ちゃんの母親、か）
English: (That would be Misty Fey... Maya's mother.)

Japanese: やがて、倉院流霊媒道の神通力も、 地に落ちる日が来ました。
English: But before long, the credibility of the Kurain Tradition hit rock bottom.

Japanese: 新しい家元・綾里　舞子が、 大きな失敗をしたからです。
English: The new Master, Mystic Misty Fey, made a terrible mistake...

Japanese: それが‥‥《ＤＬ６号事件》。
English: It was during the investigation of the DL-6 Incident.

Japanese: あの事件のあと‥‥
English: After that happened...

Japanese: 父は私たちを連れて、 おかあさまと里を捨てたのです。
English: ...our father took me and my twin sister away, leaving our mother and our home behind.

Japanese: 『もう、こんなイナカにいる理由は 何もない』‥‥そう言って‥‥。
English: He hated the place. He said it was a hick dive, and that he had no reason to stay there.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥あやめさんは この葉桜院に来たんですか？
English: And that's when you came here to Hazakura Temple?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥父の再婚相手には、 すでに勇希という娘がいました。
English: Yes. The woman my father took as his next wife already had a daughter... Valerie.

Japanese: 新しい家族の中に、私の席は もう、なかったのです。
English: I... I had no place in his new family, you see.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったんですか‥‥
English: Oh...

Japanese: それ以来‥‥おかあさまには 会っておりません。
English: And I haven't seen my mother once since then.

Japanese: 家元の座を追われ、 家族には捨てられて‥‥
English: Having the Master's seat stolen from her, and being rejected by her own family...

Japanese: 身もココロも、ボロボロになった ‥‥そんなウワサを聞きました。
English: I've heard she's been very battered, spiritually and emotionally.

Japanese: あやめさん。 ‥‥これ、見てもらえますか？
English: Could you take a look at this for me, Iris?

Japanese: いてててッ！
English: Yeow!

Japanese: それよりも、今は《錠》を なんとかするのが先よ！
English: The locks are far more important at this moment, wouldn't you agree!?

Japanese: （たしかに、そうだな‥‥）
English: (She's right...)

Japanese: （話を聞くだけで ガマンするしかないか）
English: (I'm going to have to hold off on asking her about things for now.)

Japanese: 申しわけありません。 ‥‥成歩堂さま‥‥。
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ～事件当夜のこと～
English: -- The Night of the Crime --

Japanese: そろそろ、ハッキリさせましょう。 事件当夜の、あなたの行動を‥‥
English: I think it's time you told the truth about what you were doing on that night.

Japanese: あなたは、事件が起こるまで 葉桜院の部屋にいたと言ってます。
English: You said you were in your room at Hazakura Temple the entire time.

Japanese: 今でも、その主張は 変わりませんか？
English: Do you still claim that to be the truth?

Japanese: え‥‥ええ。 まちがいありませんわ。
English: Yes... That's where I was.

Japanese: あやめさん。‥‥ぼくは、 あなたが無実だと信じています。
English: Iris... I believe you're innocent.

Japanese: だから、あなたの言うことも 信じたい。‥‥でも。
English: That's why I want to believe what you're telling me, too. But I can't.

Japanese: 事件当夜、あなたを別の場所で 目撃した人物がいるのです！
English: Because this person saw you somewhere else on the night of the murder!

Japanese: どうですか、あやめさん！
English: Well, Iris!?

Japanese: ぼくは、あなたを信じています。 だから、ぼくを信じて‥‥
English: I trust you, so you need to trust me.

Japanese: でも‥‥成歩堂さま。 私‥‥ムリです。
English: But, Mr. Wright... I can't.

Japanese: え。
English: Why not?

Japanese: そんなデタラメな証拠‥‥ 信じられませんわ！
English: I can't trust such random nonsense! It doesn't make any sense.

Japanese: （‥‥いくらなんでも、 これはなかったか‥‥）
English: (Oops. Looks like I screwed up there...)

Japanese: もちろん、ビキニさんです。
English: I'm talking about Sister Bikini, of course.

Japanese: 毘忌尼‥‥さま‥‥
English: Sister Bikini...

Japanese: 今日の裁判で、彼女は ハッキリ証言しています。
English: Her testimony in court today was very clear.

Japanese: あの晩、オバサンは《奥の院》で 修験者さまの修行を手伝ってたの。
English: That night I was helping an acolyte with her training in the Inner Temple. But...

Japanese: でも‥‥こう見えて、オバサン。 コシが悪いのよ。暴力的なまでに。
English: Well, as you can see, my back likes to act up. Violently.

Japanese: だからね。あやめにアトを任せて、 葉桜院に帰ってきたワケ。
English: So, I left Iris to help the acolyte, and returned to Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥ビキニさんは、ただ あなたを“見た”だけじゃない。
English: Sister Bikini didn't just see you, she spoke with you.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの修行について、 “会話”をしているんです。
English: You two talked about Maya's training that night.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: So you see, Iris...

Japanese: あやめさん！　あなたは、 奥の院に行ったことになります！
English: ...you were there at the Inner Temple on the night of the murder!

Japanese: ‥‥おみごとですわ、成歩堂さま。
English: That's... very impressive, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あの晩、修験者の《本行》は １０時すぎに始める予定でした。
English: The acolyte's actual training was due to start after 10 o'clock that night.

Japanese: だから、私‥‥遅れないように、 葉桜院を出たんです。
English: I left the Main Hall early, so I wouldn't be late.

Japanese: それは‥‥何時ごろの ことでしたか？
English: What time was that?

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Let me see...

Japanese: 葉桜院から奥の院まで、歩くと ２０分ほどかかりますから‥‥
English: It takes about 20 minutes to walk between the Main Hall and the Inner Temple.

Japanese: ９時４０分ごろ、だと思います。
English: So, I would've left at about 9:40 PM, I think.

Japanese: あやめさん。やっぱり あなたに、ウソは似合いませんね。
English: I'm sorry, Iris, but lying just doesn't suit you.
Huh?

Japanese: 葉桜院を９時４０分に出た‥‥ そんなことは、あり得ません！
English: Now, you say you left the Main Hall at 9:40 PM, but what you say doesn't add up with this!

Japanese: 成歩堂さま‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: あなたに、シッパイは 似合いませんわ。
English: But mistakes just don't suit you.

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: Huh?

Japanese: もう、これぐらいに しておきませんか？
English: I had hoped you would get at least this much correct.

Japanese: 私‥‥これ以上、かわいそうな 姿は見たくありませんの。
English: I hate seeing you mess up and getting all disappointed.

Japanese: （うううう‥‥意外にキビしいな）
English: (Nngh... I didn't know she could be so harsh...)

Japanese: あやめさん。いったい、 何をかくしているんですか？
English: Iris, what are you trying to hide...?

Japanese: ウソを重ねれば、それだけ “秘密”の重大さが見えてきます。
English: The more you lie about it, the more I know it's got to be something really important.

Japanese: ‥‥あなた自身が 証言しているじゃないですか。
English: You yourself testified to the contrary, don't you remember?

Japanese: 事件当夜、午後１０時‥‥ 《消灯の鐘》を鳴らした、って。
English: You said you rang the lights out bell at 10 o'clock that night.

Japanese: あっ‥‥！
English: Ah!

Japanese: だいいち、その鐘を鳴らす直前‥‥
English: Plus, only moments before you rang that bell...

Japanese: あなたは葉桜院で、 決定的な証人に目撃されている。
English: ...you were seen at the Main Hall by the most reliable witness I have.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: Who's that?

Japanese: ぼくですよ。
English: Me.

Japanese: 成歩堂さま‥‥
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: あの晩、ぼくたちは 少し話をして‥‥
English: We even spoke a little that evening.

Japanese: あなたはぼくに、コイツを くれたじゃないですか。
English: That's when you gave me this.

Japanese: ‥‥お忘れですか？
English: Surely you haven't forgotten?

Japanese: そうでした、わね‥‥
English: N-No... That's right. I remember...

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。 また１つ、ナゾが増えましたね。
English: And that brings us to another puzzling fact, Iris.

Japanese: 事件当夜の、午後１０時‥‥
English: At 10 o'clock on the night of the murder...

Japanese: あなたは、同時に ２カ所に存在したことになる！
English: ...you were seen in two different places at the same time!

Japanese: どうやら、あやめさん。 コタエを出すときが来たようです。
English: It's time you told me exactly what's been going on, Iris.

Japanese: ぼくは今までに、 ２つのことを立証しました。
English: So far, I've managed to prove two things.

Japanese: ‥‥事件当夜、あなたは 奥の院にいた。
English: First, on the night of the incident, you were at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: そして、同時に‥‥ 葉桜院で、鐘を鳴らしていた！
English: And second, at the exact same time, you were ringing the bell at Hazakura Temple!

Japanese: ‥‥このムジュンを説明する コタエは‥‥１つしかない！
English: There's only one possible explanation for this apparent impossibility!

Japanese: あやめさん。あなたの身体が １つしかない以上‥‥
English: There is only one of you, Iris.

Japanese: ２つの場所に存在することは できません。
English: You couldn't possibly have been in two places at once.

Japanese: あなたがいたのは、おそらく‥‥
English: That being the case, you were probably really at...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さま。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Yes? What is it?

Japanese: “なんですか”は こちらのセリフです。
English: That's what I wanted to ask you.

Japanese: なんですか？　今の “おそらく”というのは‥‥？
English: What was that "probably" you just came out with?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥その。
English: I, umm... Well...

Japanese: 奥の院でも、葉桜院でも‥‥ あなたは証人と会話しています。
English: The problem is, you spoke to witnesses at both the Main Hall and the Inner Temple.

Japanese: だから、どちらもウソだとは 立証しにくくて‥‥
English: It's kind of hard to disprove either sighting, but...

Japanese: いけませんね。弁護士さまが そんなことでは。
English: That's no position for a lawyer to find himself in, is it?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥ （たしなめられてしまった）
English: N-No... (She got me there!)

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、このナゾに コタエなど、ないのですわ。
English: It seems to me like there just isn't a solution to this puzzle.

Japanese: 忘れてくださいまし、 成歩堂さま。
English: It's probably best if you just forget the whole thing, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: そういうワケには 行かないんです。
English: I'm sorry, Iris, but I can't do that.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥あなたは、 ２つの場所で目撃されています。
English: On the night of the incident, you were seen in two different places at the same time.

Japanese: それならば‥‥ あなたは、“２人いた”。
English: Which means, there must have been two of you.

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えるしかありません。
English: I can't think of any other explanation, Iris.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥そんな！ どうして、そうなるのですか！
English: B-But that's crazy! How could that be!?

Japanese: わ、私が‥‥ふたりなんて。 あ、あ‥‥あり得ませんわ！
English: There's only one of me! It's impossible!

Japanese: それが、“あり得ない”か どうか‥‥？
English: Impossible? I wonder...

Japanese: あなたの動揺ぶりが、 ハッキリ物語っています。
English: The way you're trembling certainly seems to suggest otherwise.

Japanese: う‥‥っ！
English: *gulp*

Japanese: ‥‥私が、もう１人いた。 そうおっしゃるのであれば‥‥
English: You're seriously trying to suggest there's more than one of me?

Japanese: その証拠を、お見せください！ もう１人の《私》がいた、証拠を！
English: Then show me the evidence! Show me something that proves there is more than one of me!

Japanese: 成歩堂さま？
English: Err, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: “もう１人の私”というぐらい ですから‥‥
English: When you said there was "another me"...

Japanese: きっと、私と同じカオ・同じ姿を した方なのでしょうね？
English: ...I was kind of expecting someone who looked like me. You know?

Japanese: それは‥‥、たぶん そうでしょうね。
English: I... I guess so...

Japanese: その方が、私と同じカオを していると言うのですか？
English: In that case, are you saying that I look like this person?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥ハッキリ断言は できないかもしれませんが‥‥
English: Well, I can't say with 100% certainty, but...

Japanese: ‥‥断言のできる証拠を 見せていただけますか？
English: Can you show me something you are 100% certain about then...?

Japanese: （ここは‥‥よく調査を しておかなければならない‥‥）
English: (I really need to get to the bottom of this.)

Japanese: （あやめさんは、なんとしても 《秘密》を守ろうとしている）
English: (Iris is determined to hide something from me.)

Japanese: （その証拠が“決定的”でない かぎり‥‥認めないだろう）
English: (I can't drag it out of her unless I can show her some irrefutable evidence.)

Japanese: すみません。こんなコト、 私もしたくありません。‥‥でも！
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Wright. I don't mean to be uncooperative, but...

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。もう‥‥ すべて、わかっているんです。
English: I have a firm grasp of the situation now, Iris.

Japanese: あなたには、お姉さんが いるそうですね。‥‥双子の。
English: You have a sister, don't you? A twin sister, perhaps?

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: ‥‥美柳　ちなみ‥‥ ぼくも、よく知っているひとです。
English: That's right. Dahlia Hawthorne. A woman I know only too well.

Japanese: そこまで、ご存じでしたか。 さすが、成歩堂さまですね‥‥
English: I had no idea you knew of her. Very impressive, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。あの方は‥‥もう。
English: But... She's no longer...

Japanese: ええ。わかっています。
English: Yes, I know.

Japanese: 先日‥‥‥ 死刑が執行されたそうですね。
English: Her sentence was carried out recently, wasn't it...?

Japanese: おキノドクです。
English: I'm sorry.

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: あやめさん。もう 説明の必要はありませんね。
English: There's no need to explain now, is there Iris?

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥この霊場に現れた “２人目のあなた”は‥‥
English: This "second you" who was here at the temple on the night of the murder...

Japanese: この《美柳　ちなみ》だった！
English: ...it was your twin sister, Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: で、でも‥‥お忘れですか？ 成歩堂さま。
English: B-But you just said it yourself a second ago!

Japanese: 彼女は‥‥ もう、亡くなっていますのよ？
English: My sister's dead! Have you forgotten that?

Japanese: あなたこそ、お忘れですか？ あやめさん。
English: Have you forgotten this, Iris?

Japanese: ここは、霊行道場だそうですね。 あの、倉院流霊媒道の。
English: This is a channeling dojo, a training ground of the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: ‥‥だれかが、あの晩‥‥
English: That night...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみを霊媒したのです！ ‥‥あなたは、それを知っていた。
English: ...someone channeled Dahlia's spirit. And you knew about it!

Japanese: だから‥‥‥それを 《秘密》にしようとしたのです！
English: And that's the secret you've been trying to hide from me!

Japanese: きゃああああああああっ！
English: Aaaaaah!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・修験堂前
English: February 9 Inner Temple Gate

Japanese: （いよいよ、この事件も 大詰めだな）
English: (I'm finally getting to the bottom of this case.)

Japanese: （《錠》はあやめさんにまかせて、 情報を集めるんだ！）
English: (I can count on Iris to break those locks, so I should try to gather more clues.)

Japanese: （ビキニさんに、御剣‥‥ ついでに、イトノコ刑事）
English: (From Sister Bikini, Edgeworth, Gumshoe...)

Japanese: （そして‥‥春美ちゃん、か‥‥）
English: (...and Pearls...)

Japanese: おぼろ橋が見える。 よく見ると、意外に長い。
English: You can see Dusky Bridge from here, and it feels like an unusually long bridge to me.

Japanese: これじゃあ、ワイヤーを伝って 向こう岸にわたるのはムリだ。
English: I guess the gorge is too wide to cross with a piece of rope or wire.

Japanese: つまり、事件当夜から今日まで、 ここに出入りできた人間は、ない。
English: In other words, no one has crossed it in either direction since the night of the murder.

Japanese: 奥の院の中庭に細工することは 不可能だった、ということだ。
English: Which means that scene in the Inner Temple's garden couldn't have been set up by someone.

Japanese: おぼろ橋を補強するための ワイヤーがかかっていた金具だ。
English: That's an anchor for one of the wires that used to hold up the bridge.

Japanese: 橋が燃えたときに、 切れてしまったようだ。
English: I guess it must have snapped during the fire.

Japanese: お地蔵さまがズラリと 並んでいる。
English: Look at all the Buddha statues lined up along this path.

Japanese: ‥‥お供えのおだんごが １個、なくなっているな。
English: Looks like one of the cakes someone left as an offering is missing, though.

Japanese: ハラをすかしたイトノコ刑事の 魔の手が伸びたらしい。
English: I guess Gumshoe couldn't resist, the poor guy.

Japanese: ‥‥この焼却炉‥‥
English: There's something strange about this incinerator.

Japanese: 最初に見たときは、 たしか‥‥雪がつもっていた。
English: I'm sure there was snow on it the first time I saw it.

Japanese: フタも開きっぱなしだし、 ここで何かが燃やされたようだ。
English: That, and the door's open. Someone's been burning something, that's for sure.

Japanese: ただ‥‥中に手がかりは 残されていない。
English: It's a shame there isn't a trace of what it was left inside.

Japanese: この奥の修験洞に、 真宵ちゃんがいるはずだ。
English: Maya has got to be inside the Sacred Cavern. She's just got to be.

Japanese: 何もできないのがツラいけど‥‥ 一刻も早く、助けてあげたい。
English: I hate feeling so useless. It's driving me nuts.

Japanese: そのときのために‥‥手がかりを 探しておかないとな。
English: But I need to look for clues so that I'm ready when we finally get her out of there.

Japanese: 《奥の院》と書かれているの だろう。‥‥たぶん。
English: I can't read it very well, but I think that sign says, "Inner Temple" on it.

Japanese: ‥‥いや。《修験堂》かも しれないな。
English: Or maybe it says, "Training Hall".

Japanese: ‥‥いやいや。意表をついて 《おぼろ小橋》とか。
English: No. Maybe it's not that at all. Maybe it says, "The Bridge of Dawn".

Japanese: さっさとシゴトをしよう。
English: ...I really should get back to work!

Japanese: この小さなトビラは、 中庭に通じている。
English: On the other side of that little gate is the garden.

Japanese: 中庭では、御剣が しょんぼりしているはずだ。
English: Edgeworth is probably still in there, crying softly to himself.

Japanese: ‥‥話だけは、キッチリ 聞いておかないとな。
English: I guess we better hug it out and talk about what he's uncovered so far.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・中庭
English: February 9 Inner Temple Garden

Japanese: ‥‥警官たちが、オドオドと 捜査をつづけている。
English: The cops are still combing the place. They look pretty nervous...

Japanese: ものすごく、やりにくそうだ。 そりゃそうだろう。
English: I'd be nervous, too. It's got to be a tough job.

Japanese: ‥‥イミもなく、こんな目で ニラみつけられているのだから。
English: Especially with someone giving you the evil eye the whole time.

Japanese: ぶつぶつ‥‥ ‥‥私としたことが‥‥ッ！
English: *mutter, mutter* How could I have done that...?

Japanese: （まだ、気にしてるみたいだな）
English: (Wow. I can't believe it's still bothering him.)
Edgeworth...?
Aaaah!

Japanese: おい！　どこへ行くんだよ！
English: Hey! Don't you dare run away!

Japanese: ‥‥何しに来た！
English: ...What do you want, Wright?

Japanese: な、“何しに”と言われても‥‥
English: What do I want?

Japanese: こんな私を見て、笑いに来たのか！ ‥‥笑いたければ、笑うがいい。
English: If you came here to laugh at me, then get on with it.

Japanese: どうした！　さあ、笑えよ！
English: Go on! Laugh away!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ コイツも変わらないな）
English: (*sigh* I was ready to hug it out with him, but he's just the same, prideful Edgeworth.)

Japanese: そういえば、おまえ‥‥ 署に戻っていたんだよな？
English: You went back to the Criminal Affairs Department, right?

Japanese: あやめさんのこと‥‥ 《調べたいことがある》って。
English: You said you wanted to look into something concerning Iris.

Japanese: う、うム‥‥。
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: ‥‥おかげで、イヤなことを 思い出してしまった‥‥。
English: ...And thanks to what I found, I was reminded of something terrible.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣。ひとつ、 知らないことがあるみたいだな。
English: It seems you're not aware of one other connection yet, Edgeworth.

Japanese: なんだ？
English: And what is that?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみとあやめさんの 母親のことだ。
English: It's about Iris and Dahlia Hawthorne's mother.

Japanese: ははおや‥‥？
English: What's their mother got to do with any of this?

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　キミ子、という。
English: She's Morgan Fey.

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道、分家の霊媒師だ。
English: A spirit medium from a branch family of the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道、だと‥‥？
English: Did you say, "Kurain Channeling Technique"...?

Japanese: し‥‥知ってるのか？
English: D-Do you know something about it?

Japanese: モチロンだ。 ‥‥あの、インチキ霊媒師！
English: Oh, yes. I know it's connected with that fraudulent spirit medium!

Japanese: （インチキ‥‥？）
English: (Fraudulent...?)

Japanese: １７年前‥‥ もう１つの、悪夢だ。
English: I was involved in another nightmare, 17 years ago.

Japanese: 私が巻きこまれた、あの事件は 手がかりがいっさい、なかった。
English: I was caught up in the middle of a murder investigation. The police didn't have any leads.

Japanese: 打つ手をなくした警察は‥‥ ある霊媒師を連れてきた。
English: They were stumped... And that's when they called her in.

Japanese: “家元”と名乗ったその女は、 被害者の霊を呼び出した‥‥
English: She was a very famous spirit medium and the "Master" of her channeling school.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: But you know what happened?

Japanese: その結果！　無実の男が、 罪に問われることになった！
English: As a result of her efforts, an innocent man was accused of murder!

Japanese: その霊媒師のチカラが、 インチキだったからだ！
English: She and her powers! They were all fraudulent!
...Edgeworth...

Japanese: 今でも、警察の資料室に行けば、 その記録は眠っている。
English: Go to the Police Records Room. It's all in there.

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》という 分類ナンバーでな！
English: All you have to do is check the DL-6 Incident case file and you'll know!

Japanese: （そうだった‥‥）
English: (Of course... How could I forget...)

Japanese: （こいつは、あの事件の 被害者だったんだな‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth was the victim in that case.)

Japanese: ‥‥御剣。 いつか、おまえにもわかるよ。
English: Edgeworth... You'll understand someday...

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道が、 インチキかどうか‥‥
English: And then, you'll see that the Kurain Channeling Technique is real...

Japanese: ‥‥キミも気づいたか。 《マヨイ》‥‥
English: I suppose you've noticed that Maya's name is written on this lantern.

Japanese: さすがにこれは、 気づかない方がむずかしいよ。
English: It's pretty hard to miss, you know.

Japanese: 被害者・天流斎　エリスの血で 書かれている。
English: It's written in Ms. Deauxnim's blood.

Japanese: おそらく、犯人に追いつめられた 彼女は、灯ろうを背にして立った。
English: She was probably forced up against the lantern by the killer.

Japanese: そしてそのまま、後ろ手で 灯ろうに文字を書いたのだ。
English: Then with her back against the lantern, she wrote that name with her finger.

Japanese: だから‥‥文字が逆さまに なったわけだな。
English: That's why it's written upside-down.

Japanese: 被害者が‥‥エリス先生が これを書いた、というのか‥‥？
English: You think the victim wrote this herself?

Japanese: そう考えるのが自然だろう。
English: Isn't that the obvious conclusion?

Japanese: ‥‥どうして‥‥ 真宵ちゃんの名前が‥‥
English: But why would she write Maya's name?

Japanese: その理由について‥‥ キミと話す気にはなれないな。
English: I don't think that's something I want to discuss with you.

Japanese: ‥‥雪の中から、 《お守り》がのぞいている。
English: There's a charm or something poking out from the snow.

Japanese: （ずいぶん古いものだ。 革のヒモがついているな‥‥）
English: (It looks pretty old. There's a leather cord tied to it, too.)

Japanese: 被害者のものであることは わかっている。
English: It belonged to the victim. We're sure of that now.

Japanese: 現場検証が終わったら回収する。 勝手に持っていくなよ。
English: It's going in the evidence room once the investigation is over, so hands off, Wright.

Japanese: そんなコト、するわけないだろう！
English: You don't need to tell me that!

Japanese: ‥‥さっき、矢張がスキを見て 持ち去ろうとしたのだ。
English: Well, our dear friend Larry just tried to steal it when no one was looking.

Japanese: （‥‥あいつ、エリス先生を ソンケイしてたからな‥‥）
English: (He probably just wanted something to remember his esteemed teacher by...)

Japanese: 小さな灯ろうに、 小さなししおどし‥‥
English: A little lantern, a little statue...

Japanese: 小さいけど、リッパな庭園だ。
English: It's small, but the whole place is incredibly well-kept.

Japanese: １つだけ‥‥気になることがある。
English: There's just one thing that's troubling me.

Japanese: 他のものは雪をかぶり、 こおりついているのに‥‥
English: Everything else is covered in snow or frozen over...

Japanese: この、ちょうずばちに張った 氷だけが、キレイに割られている。
English: ...but the ice in this bowl has been broken by someone.

Japanese: 刑事のだれかが、カオでも 洗ったんじゃないか？
English: Maybe one of the detectives washed their face in it or something?

Japanese: 刑事をバカにするなッ！ 殺人現場で、だれがそんなコトを！
English: Don't be ridiculous, Wright! No detective would be that careless at a crime scene!

Japanese: （怒られた‥‥）
English: (He's really angry... I'd better not say it was Gumshoe or his salary's gonna pay...)

Japanese: “かがり火”だ。
English: These are torches of the naked flame variety.

Japanese: 街灯のない山の中だから、 大切な明かりなのだろう。
English: I guess you need all the light you can get up here in the mountains.

Japanese: 刑事の報告によると‥‥ 事件当夜、燃やされたらしい。
English: Accordingly to Detective Gumshoe, they were lit on the night of the murder.

Japanese: まあ、真宵ちゃんの修行が あったわけだからなあ。
English: Well, Maya was training up here, so there's nothing strange about that.

Japanese: だが‥‥
English: Except...

Japanese: 修行では、かがり火は 燃やさないそうだ。
English: Apparently, they don't light these torches for training sessions.
Huh?

Japanese: 修行は、よけいな光をきらう‥‥ 私は、そう聞いたぞ。
English: I heard that too much light isn't conducive to spiritual training.

Japanese: （‥‥そうなのか‥‥）
English: (Oh... I didn't know that...)

Japanese: なんで、ここだけ 雪がないんだろう。
English: Why's this the only area with no snow?

Japanese: だれかが持ち去った‥‥ そうとしか考えられない。
English: Someone must have cleared it away. What other explanation is there?

Japanese: 私も“もしや”と思って‥‥
English: Of course, I played the "What If?" game as well...

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事を問いつめたが、 ‥‥どうやら、彼ではないらしい。
English: But when I asked our trusty bungler, Detective Gumshoe, he said it wasn't him.

Japanese: （そりゃそうだろう‥‥）
English: (I suppose Edgeworth has a point...)

Japanese: （だれかが、雪を持ち去った‥‥ 何か、イミがあるんだろうな）
English: (Whoever cleared the snow away must've had a reason to do so.)

Japanese: （今は、見当もつかないけど）
English: (I just can't imagine what that reason could be.)

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥まだ、 ダメなのか。地震‥‥
English: So... I guess you still haven't gotten over your fear of earthquakes...

Japanese: ああ‥‥。さすがに、 悪夢は見なくなったが‥‥
English: No... Thankfully, my nightmares have stopped.

Japanese: 今でも、足元が揺れると 息ができなくなる。
English: But still, if the ground gives even the slightest tremor, I find myself short of breath.

Japanese: （‥‥今から、１７年前‥‥ ぼくたちが、まだ小学生のころ）
English: (...17 years ago... when we were little school kids at the same elementary school...)

Japanese: （御剣　怜侍は、ある 殺人事件に巻きこまれたんだ）
English: (Edgeworth found himself in the middle of a murder...)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判所を襲った、大地震‥‥ それが、すべての始まりだった。
English: It all started with that big quake that hit the courthouse...

Japanese: 私は、弁護士である父とともに、 エレベータに閉じこめられた。
English: Yes. I was stuck in the elevator with my father, who was a defense lawyer.

Japanese: ‥‥酸素が失われ、うすれゆく 意識の中で、あの事件は起こった。
English: We were deprived of oxygen and we passed out. That's when it happened.

Japanese: ‥‥１発の、銃声‥‥ それが、すべての終わりだった。
English: That single gunshot... shattered my whole life...

Japanese: あの日、あの地震によって‥‥ 何もかも、失ってしまった。
English: I lost everything that day... All because of that earthquake.

Japanese: 夢も、家族も‥‥ そして、自分自身も。
English: My dreams, my family... and myself.

Japanese: もう、１７年も前のことだ。 それに‥‥
English: It's been more than 17 years now...

Japanese: あの事件なら、解決しただろう？ 今から、３年前に。
English: And that case was finally resolved 3 years ago, right?

Japanese: ‥‥わかっているさ！ 自分でも、そのつもりでいた。
English: You think I don't know that!? I was there! But...

Japanese: それだけに、ショックだ。 ‥‥こんなミスをするとは、な。
English: It was such a shock. I never imagined I could be so wrong about myself and my life...

Japanese: 私のために弁護士の道を選び、 助けてくれたキミにも‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Wright... There's nothing else I can say.

Japanese: ‥‥申しわけない。
English: Not after you chose to become a lawyer for my sake... And not after you saved me...

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ ずいぶん弱気になっちまったな）
English: (Edgeworth... You're stronger than you think, so no more of this self-pity, OK...?)

Japanese: ‥‥ずいぶん、念入りだな。 中庭の捜査‥‥
English: You guys are putting a lot of effort into the investigation of the garden here, huh...

Japanese: 本当の殺害現場がここである 可能性が高いからな。
English: There's a high chance this is the actual scene of the crime. That's why.

Japanese: 血文字と、この護符のせいか。
English: You mean because of the writing in blood and the talisman in the snow?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだ。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: いずれも、事件が起こってからの 細工は不可能だ。
English: As you know, those things couldn't have been planted here after the murder.

Japanese: まさか‥‥疑っているわけじゃ ないだろうな。
English: But surely... you don't suspect...

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんのこと。
English: ...Maya, do you?

Japanese: すべてを疑うのが我々のシゴトだ。 真宵くんも‥‥、あやめさんも。
English: We have to treat everyone as a suspect - Maya, as well as Iris. It's our job, Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女に会ったとき、 気になったことがあった。
English: There was something that bothered me about her from the moment we met.

Japanese: “どこかで見たことがある”‥‥ そう思ったのだ。‥‥いや。
English: I felt like I'd seen her somewhere before. No... Wait...

Japanese: “どこかで”と言うより‥‥ “法廷で”‥‥と言うべきか。
English: Not "somewhere"... I felt like I'd seen her in court before.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、署に戻って 調べてきたのか。
English: So you went back to the Criminal Affairs Department to look for her file?

Japanese: 私の扱った、すべての 事件のデータを、な。
English: Yes. I checked over every case file I've ever worked on.

Japanese: ‥‥やはり、思いちがいでは なかった。
English: And I was right.

Japanese: 私は、彼女のカオを 見たことがあった。６年ほど前に。
English: I had seen her face before, six years ago.
Six years ago...?

Japanese: ‥‥初めての法廷だった。 私にとって、最悪の事件だ。
English: It was my first appearance in court, and as cases go, it was my worse nightmare.

Japanese: そ、それで！ 《彼女》というのは‥‥
English: So!? Who is she...?

Japanese: すまないが、成歩堂。
English: I'm sorry, Wright.

Japanese: その人物に関するデータを、 外部にもらすことはできない。
English: I can't give that information away to a member of the general public.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしてだよ！ 事件を解決するためだぞ！
English: What!? Why not? It might be the crucial piece of the puzzle that solves this case.

Japanese: 私の知っている《彼女》は、 宝石商の父を持つ娘だ。
English: The woman I knew was the daughter of a jewelry store owner.

Japanese: 葉桜院で育った あやめさんとの接点は、ない。
English: She had nothing to do with Iris and Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥それに‥‥
English: And neither did the case.

Japanese: その人物は、この事件とは 無関係なのだ。
English: No, that woman is completely unrelated to this murder.

Japanese: ‥‥“無関係”だって‥‥？
English: Unrelated?

Japanese: これは、絶対的なジジツ として断言できる。
English: Yes. I can say that with complete confidence.

Japanese: （バカな！　《彼女》が 無関係なハズがない！）
English: (You're wrong, Edgeworth! She's totally related to this case!)

Japanese: （御剣に、わからせるんだ！）
English: (I need to fill Edgeworth in.)

Japanese: （あやめさんと‥‥御剣の知って いる《彼女》との接点を！）
English: (I need to explain the connection between Iris and the woman Edgeworth knew.)

Japanese: 今から、６年ほど前‥‥ 初めて扱った事件だった。
English: It was my first court case, six years ago.

Japanese: 私がミジュクだったせいで‥‥ 被告人を、死なせてしまったのだ。
English: I was a greenhorn, and due to my inexperience, the defendant died.

Japanese: 尾並田　美散さん、か‥‥
English: You're talking about Terry Fawles, right?

Japanese: ‥‥‥！　し、知っているのか？ あの事件を‥‥
English: Y-You know about that case...!?

Japanese: “あやめさん”と“美柳　ちなみ” ‥‥ぼくも気になっていた。
English: You're not the only one who noticed something about Iris and Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: だから‥‥６年前の、千尋さんの ファイルを調べたんだ。
English: I checked one of Mia's old files from six years ago.

Japanese: そう‥‥。あのときの証人が 美柳　ちなみ、だ。
English: Yes, Dahlia was a key witness in that case.

Japanese: 尾並田　美散を協力させて 狂言誘拐をくわだて‥‥
English: Dahlia and Terry Fawles conspired together to stage a fake kidnapping 11 years ago.

Japanese: 宝石商の父親から、２億円の ダイヤの原石を奪った‥‥
English: They stole a jewel worth 2 million dollars from Dahlia's father, a jeweler.

Japanese: そして、その５年後‥‥
English: And five years after that...

Japanese: こんどは、口封じのために お姉さんの美柳　勇希を殺害した。
English: ...she murdered her own sister, Valerie Hawthorne, to keep her from talking.

Japanese: “お姉さん”‥‥か。
English: Her sister?

Japanese: 美柳　勇希と美柳　ちなみには、 血のつながりはない。
English: Well, her stepsister, actually. They weren't blood-related.

Japanese: 勇希は、父親が再婚した相手の ひとり娘だったようだ。
English: Valerie was the only daughter of Dahlia's father's second wife.

Japanese: ‥‥そう、だったのか‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: （そこから‥‥ぼくの知っている 《彼女》につながるわけだな）
English: (And this is when she entered my life...)

Japanese: （このぼくを殺そうとした “彼女”に‥‥）
English: (The woman who tried to kill me.)

Japanese: ‥‥尾並田さんが亡くなったあと、 美柳　ちなみがどうなったか‥‥？
English: So after Terry Fawles died, what happened to Dahlia?

Japanese: そいつは、調べなかったのか？
English: Did you check that out?

Japanese: その必要はない。
English: There is no need.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみは、この事件とは 無関係なのだからな。
English: Like I said, Dahlia isn't connected with this case.

Japanese: なぜ‥‥ そう言い切れるんだ？
English: Why are you so sure about that?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなことだ。
English: It's simple.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみは‥‥ すでに死んでいる。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne is dead.
What?

Japanese: そう。‥‥彼女はもう この世には、いないのだ‥‥。
English: Well, her metabolic processes are a matter of interest only to historians, so to speak.

Japanese: どういうコトだ、御剣！ 美柳　ちなみが、“死んでいる”？
English: What do you mean by, "Dahlia Hawthorne is dead", Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 今回、初めて知ったよ。 ‥‥成歩堂。
English: I only just discovered this, Wright, but...

Japanese: キミの、学生時代の“事件”を。
English: I never knew about the murder case you were involved in during your college years.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみは有罪判決を受けた。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne was found guilty...

Japanese: 綾里　千尋の、執念によって‥‥
English: ...thanks to the persistence of Mia Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥ぐ‥‥ぐぐううう‥‥‥‥
English: ...Grr... Nngghh... Hnnn...

Japanese: アヤサト　チヒロ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ アヤサト　チヒロオオォォ‥‥‥
English: MIA FEY...! MIA... FEYYYY...!!

Japanese: （あのときの、彼女‥‥）
English: (At the time... Dahlia...)

Japanese: （まさに、悪魔のような 形相だった‥‥）
English: (It's like she was possessed by a demon or something...)

Japanese: ‥‥あの判決から ６年たった、今年。
English: It's been six years since that guilty verdict was handed down...

Japanese: ついに、執行されたのだ。
English: ...and her sentence was finally carried out.

Japanese: 彼女の死刑が‥‥な。 つい、先月のことだ。
English: She was executed... last month.
E-Executed?

Japanese: キミには、いろいろなイミで ショックかもしれないな。
English: I'm sure that's a bit of a shock for you, Wright. And for more reasons than one.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。 これで、わかっただろう？
English: But do you understand now?

Japanese: 彼女が、事件に関わるなど あり得ない。
English: She can't possibly be connected with this case.

Japanese: 死者は、よみがえることなど ないのだから。
English: She's dead, and once someone is dead, there is no way to revive them.

Japanese: （死者は、よみがえらない‥‥）
English: (There's no way to revive the dead...?)

Japanese: （本当に、そうなのだろうか‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I wonder...)

Japanese: ‥‥《伝説の検事》だそうだな。
English: He's something of a legend, I hear.

Japanese: 他人ゴトみたいに言うなよ。 なんか知らないのか？　彼のこと。
English: Don't say it like you don't know anything about him! Tell me what you know.

Japanese: ぬうう‥‥それが、妙なのだ。
English: It's a bit strange, actually.

Japanese: 彼が優秀な人物ならば、とっくに 私の耳に入っているはずだ。
English: If he really was anything special, I should have heard about him by now.

Japanese: 研修中の検事たちのデータも、 私のところへ届くのだから。
English: I'm always well-informed about the rookies coming into the Prosecutor's Office, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥と、いうことは‥‥
English: Then you mean...

Japanese: 優秀じゃない‥‥のかな。 ゴドー検事は。
English: ...he isn't anything special?

Japanese: フッ‥‥。それは、キミが よく知っているだろう。
English: Hmph. You should already know the answer to that.

Japanese: ただ‥‥ もう１つ、別の可能性がある。
English: Although, there is one other possibility...

Japanese: な、なんだ？
English: What's that?

Japanese: ‥‥別のルートから、 検事になった場合だ。
English: He could have come into the profession from a different channel.

Japanese: 別のルート‥‥？
English: What different channel?

Japanese: たとえば‥‥
English: There's more than one way to become a prosecutor, Wright.

Japanese: 弁護士から転身した、とか。
English: Perhaps he started off as a defense attorney, but decided it wasn't for him?

Japanese: 弁護士から‥‥ （そんなことができるのか‥‥）
English: A defense attorney...? (I didn't even know you could switch sides like that...)

Japanese: ‥‥我々の世界で、彼女の名前を 知らないものは少ないだろう。
English: One of the biggest names in the world of law, as I'm sure you'll agree.

Japanese: 幸か不幸か、私が彼女と法廷で 会ったのは、一度きりだったが。
English: For better or for worse, we were only destined to meet once in the courtroom.

Japanese: （そのときの記録‥‥ 読ませてもらったよ）
English: (I know. I read all about it.)

Japanese: 私にとっても、彼女にとっても ‥‥あれは、初めての法廷だった。
English: It was the first time for the both of us. Two novices, head to head.

Japanese: あのときの、やるせないキモチ‥‥ 今もまだ、忘れられない。
English: I can still remember how downhearted I felt after that trial ended the way it did...

Japanese: （千尋さんも そうだったのかな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if Mia felt the same way, too.)

Japanese: 真宵くんのことは よく知らないのだが‥‥
English: I don't know Maya Fey very well.

Japanese: いつも大変な目にあっている ような印象がある。
English: But it seems to me that the girl is always landing herself in trouble.

Japanese: ‥‥おおむね まちがってないよ、その印象。
English: It seems to me that you know her better than you think!

Japanese: ブジであってくれればいいが。
English: I do sincerely hope she's going to be alright...

Japanese: ‥‥さっき、うどんを ごちそうしておいた。
English: I was just with Detective Gumshoe. I treated him to some pasta.

Japanese: イトノコさんの給料、超人的に 安いからな。おまえのせいで。
English: You owe him that much! After all, it's your fault his salary is so low!

Japanese: わ‥‥私のせい、なのだろうか。
English: H-How's it my fault?

Japanese: ごちそうするなら、もっと マシなものにしてやれよ。
English: And look, if you're going to buy the guy lunch, at least get him something decent, OK?

Japanese: イトノコさんの主食だぞ。 素うどんは。
English: Gumshoe practically lives on pasta and noodles already.

Japanese: ‥‥いちおう、天ぷらを つけておいた。
English: Well I did order some extra parmesan cheese on top.

Japanese: 泣きながらすすりこむ姿を見て、 なぜか私も、少し泣けたな。
English: He was crying tears of joy as he dug into it. It was quite moving.

Japanese: （‥‥給料を上げて あげればいいのに）
English: (...All you have to do is give the poor guy a raise...)

Japanese: 事件の夜から、こちら側に 閉じこめられていたらしいな。
English: It seems Pearl was trapped on this side of the river on the night of the murder.

Japanese: 何か、話を聞けたのか？
English: Did she tell you anything?

Japanese: ‥‥いいや。
English: Listen, Wright...

Japanese: なにぶん、小さい子だ。 ムリに聞き出すワケにはいかぬ。
English: She's just a kid. I can't force things out of her.

Japanese: この子のことは、キミに まかせるしかないようだ。
English: You'll have to be the one to find out what she knows.

Japanese: 彼女の証言が、すべてウソならば ‥‥そう思うことがある。
English: It's crossed my mind once or twice that her testimony might be a bunch of lies.

Japanese: まあ‥‥ビキニさんの証言、 かなりムリがあるからなあ。
English: There certainly are a lot of problems with it.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: The thing is...

Japanese: 彼女に関するかぎり、 ウソをつく理由はない。
English: ...as far as I can tell, she has no reason to deceive us.

Japanese: 彼女は、奥の院で被告人と会い、 その後、殺人現場を目撃したのだ。
English: I believe she did meet the defendant at the Inner Temple, and witnessed the murder, too.

Japanese: ‥‥葉桜院の境内で。
English: The one in the Hazakura Temple courtyard, in any case.

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、 身元はわかっていない。
English: Another victim we know nothing about. Same as always.

Japanese: 何か知っているのなら‥‥ 教えてほしいな、成歩堂。
English: I hope you'll tell me if you know something about our mystery woman, Wright.

Japanese: （葉桜院にとって、エリスさんは ただの泊まり客じゃなかった）
English: (I know that Elise Deauxnim was no ordinary visitor to the temple.)

Japanese: （でも‥‥彼女の正体は、 こっちにとっては武器だ）
English: (But her true identity could be a useful weapon for the defense.)

Japanese: （カンタンに教えるわけには いかないな‥‥）
English: (And I can't give that away just like that...)

Japanese: おさないころ、葉桜院に 引き取られたらしい。
English: Apparently, Iris was taken in by the sisters here at the temple when she was a child.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女の“それ以前”に ついては、まだ調査中だ。
English: However, we still don't know much about her past, so we're looking into it.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (That reminds me...)

Japanese: （御剣が署に戻ったのは、 彼女のことを調べるためだった）
English: (Edgeworth went back to the Criminal Affairs Department to look up some stuff on her...)

Japanese: （話を聞いておかないとな‥‥）
English: (I should ask him what he found.)

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス先生の お弟子さん、だそうだ。
English: Apparently he had become something of an apprentice to Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: ‥‥そうらしいな。
English: Yes. Something like that.

Japanese: さっき、似顔絵を 描いてもらった。
English: He did this portrait for me earlier.

Japanese: ‥‥そいつはよかった。
English: That's great...

Japanese: これ以上、この男に対する コメントは、ひかえさせてもらう。
English: Let's just leave it at that about Larry, alright?

Japanese: （やっぱり、ハラを立ててるみたい だな。‥‥今日の法廷）
English: (Looks like someone's still mad at the Butz for his performance in court today.)

Japanese: 彼女に会うのは、 私も１年ぶりなのだが‥‥
English: It's been more than a year since I last saw Franziska myself.

Japanese: どうやら、キミに 会いたがっていたようだ。
English: But it seems that she's been dying to see you again.

Japanese: 狩魔検事が、ぼくに‥‥？
English: She really said that...?

Japanese: キミにゴチソウしたいらしい。 彼女も、ウデを上げたからな。
English: I believe she wants to eat you for breakfast! Or to put it another way, whip you silly.

Japanese: ‥‥ムチの。
English: Literally and metaphorically.

Japanese: え。
English: Oh.

Japanese: この国のみやげ話に、せいぜい たたかれてやってくれ。
English: Go on, let her have her way! It would make for a great anecdote about my trip!

Japanese: やだよ！
English: Forget it, Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥理想の審理のありかたを 知るには、学ぶべきコトが多い。
English: If you want to know how to run the perfect trial, there's a lot to study and learn.

Japanese: 今、そんなことを 研究しているのか。
English: Oh, is that what you're studying about right now?

Japanese: ５カ国の法廷の検事席に、 助手として立ってきた。
English: I've sat at the prosecutor's bench for trials in five different countries now.

Japanese: まず語学から学ばなければ ならない。なかなか大変だ。
English: The first thing you must do is gain complete mastery of the language. It's quite a task.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくなら、それだけで 一生かかるな‥‥）
English: (I'd never get anywhere then! It's taken me a lifetime to sort of master one language!)

Japanese: なあ、御剣‥‥ 知っていたか？
English: Hey, Edgeworth...

Japanese: あやめさんには‥‥ 双子のお姉さんがいたんだ。
English: Did you know that Iris had a twin sister...?

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！ ‥‥ふ、双子‥‥
English: WHAT!? A-A twin...?

Japanese: ‥‥バカなッ！ まちがいないのか！
English: You can't be serious!

Japanese: ビキニさんから聞いたんだ。 ‥‥でも‥‥
English: Sister Bikini told me. But...

Japanese: その“お姉さん”については、 名前さえも知らないらしい。
English: The problem is, she didn't know the name of this twin.

Japanese: ‥‥私が調べた記録には、姉妹の ことなど書かれていなかった‥‥
English: There was nothing about Iris having a sister in the files I checked.

Japanese: おさないころ、あやめさんは 葉桜院にあずけられて‥‥
English: Well, Iris was taken in by the temple when she was really young.

Japanese: お姉さんの方は、宝石商の父親に 育てられたらしい。
English: Apparently her sister was raised by her father. A jeweler, I think.

Japanese: ほ‥‥宝石商、だと‥‥！
English: A jeweler!?

Japanese: 成歩堂。私は‥‥
English: Wright, I...

Japanese: 私は、その“双子の姉”を 知っているかもしれない。
English: I might just know who this twin sister of hers is.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 そう言うだろうと思ったよ。
English: I had a feeling you'd say that.

Japanese: 彼女の名前は‥‥
English: Let me guess...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみ、だろう？
English: Her name is Dahlia Hawthorne, right?

Japanese: そのとおりだ。
English: Yes, exactly.

Japanese: 聞かせてもらおうか。 ‥‥彼女のこと‥‥
English: Please tell me what you know about her, Edgeworth... Please.

Japanese: ‥‥きみも、ショックだった だろうな。
English: I know it must be quite a shock for you to find out like this.

Japanese: まさか、自分のコイビトが‥‥
English: I mean, it must be hard to admit that someone you loved has ended up like...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいんだ。
English: Let's just forget it, OK?

Japanese: （‥‥バカなコトだとは わかっている）
English: (I know it's crazy...)

Japanese: （でも‥‥ぼくは、 まだ、信じている）
English: (But I still believe in her.)

Japanese: （ぼくの知っている彼女が、 あんなことをするはずがない！）
English: (The girl I dated couldn't have done something like that. She couldn't have!)

Japanese: 弁護士バッジ、か。 ‥‥おもしろいものだな。
English: Is that your attorney's badge? Interesting.

Japanese: まさか、キミがこれを つけることになるなんて‥‥
English: I never imagined I'd ever see you wearing one of these, Wright.

Japanese: おまえのせいだぞ、御剣。
English: It's because of you that I became one, Edgeworth.

Japanese: もちろん、 後悔はしていないけど。
English: Not that I have any regrets. I really don't.

Japanese: （‥‥礼なら、聞こえるように 言えよな‥‥）
English: (If you're trying to say "thanks", you can start by saying it a bit louder...)

Japanese: あやめさんから もらったそうだな。
English: Iris gave that to you, didn't she?

Japanese: これをかぶっていたおかげで、 熱が早く下がった気がするよ。
English: Yes, and it really works! It helped my fever go down quicker. I'm sure of it.

Japanese: ‥‥ミドリ色のカオに、満面の 冷やアセを浮かべて言われてもな。
English: So you say! But your face is as white as a sheet, and sweat is running down your cheek!

Japanese: とにかく‥‥キミと彼女の あいだには、何かあるようだな。
English: Anyway, it seems to me like there's something going on between the two of you.

Japanese: じつは‥‥
English: Actually...

Japanese: ぼくにも、よくわからないんだ。 あやめさんのことが‥‥
English: I don't know exactly why, but there's something about Iris that just tugs at me.

Japanese: よく知ってるような‥‥ まったく、他人であるような‥‥
English: I can't figure out whether I feel like I know her, or don't know her at all.

Japanese: なぜか、ハッキリしない。
English: It's really weird.

Japanese: ‥‥‥ぬうう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: そんなものを見せるなッ！
English: Get that piece of filth out of my face!

Japanese: やっぱり、怒ってるのか？ ‥‥御剣。
English: You're still mad, huh, Edgeworth?

Japanese: 矢張のコトは、少しは 知っているつもりだ。
English: I know a thing or two about Larry Butz, just like you.

Japanese: アイツはふざけた男だが‥‥ こんな想像力はない。
English: We both know he likes to act up, but this? Even he couldn't make something like this up.

Japanese: これを描いた以上‥‥ ヤツは、目撃したのだろう。
English: If this is what he drew, then... this is what he saw.

Japanese: この、信じられない光景を。
English: As unbelievable as it may seem...

Japanese: ‥‥だろうね。 ぼくもそう思う。
English: I know. That's what I think, too.

Japanese: まったく‥‥毎回まいかい、 よけいなコトをする男だ！
English: Every time! Every single time I see that guy, he's in it up to his neck!

Japanese: （やっぱり、怒ってる じゃないか‥‥）
English: (I knew it. Edgeworth's still mad as a hornet at Larry.)

Japanese: 御剣。こいつなんだけど‥‥
English: What do you think about this, Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥悪いが、成歩堂。
English: Sorry, Wright...

Japanese: キミと私は、検事と弁護士の カンケイにある。
English: You're a defense attorney, and I'm a prosecutor.

Japanese: 事件に関する証拠について 語ることはできないな。
English: It wouldn't be right for us to discuss things so intimately.

Japanese: （‥‥今日の法廷では 弁護席に立ってたクセに‥‥）
English: (But you were acting for the defense in the trial today!)
